seoTitle: Thornville vs Three Rivers — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Thornville and Three Rivers. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Thornville vs Three Rivers: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thornville and Three Rivers.
heading: Thornville vs Three Rivers — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thornville, Queensland and Three Rivers, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Three Rivers has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Thornville (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thornville or Three Rivers safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thornville has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Three Rivers scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thornville scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Three Rivers scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thornville or Three Rivers?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Three Rivers has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
